controls.control_objective: To ensure strengthen their systems/ mechanisms to monitor availability of cash in atms and ensure timely replenishment to prevent cash- outs and thereby avoid i
controls.control_description:
1.2 Since issuance of banknotes is facilitated through ATMs on a large scale, banks/
White Label ATM Operators (WLAOs) shall strengthen their systems/ mechanisms to
monitor availability of cash in ATMs and ensure timely replenishment to prevent cash-
outs and thereby avoid inconvenience to members of the public.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to strengthen their systems/ mechanisms to monitor availability of cash in atms and ensure timely replenishment to prevent cash- outs and thereby avoid i

controls.control_objective: To ensure ensure that all their branches provide facility for exchange of notes and coins not only to their customers but also others
controls.control_description:
1.3 Banks shall ensure that all their branches provide facility for exchange of notes
and coins not only to their customers but also others.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to ensure that all their branches provide facility for exchange of notes and coins not only to their customers but also others

controls.control_objective: To ensure exchange mutilated and imperfect notes at their option
controls.control_description:
Small Finance Banks (up to two
years from the commencement of their banking business) and Payment Banks may
exchange mutilated and imperfect notes at their option.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to exchange mutilated and imperfect notes at their option

controls.control_objective: To ensure be kept at the counters and made available to the customers
controls.control_description:
1.4 Considering that handling coins packed in sachets of 100 pieces each or small
value-based sachets would be more convenient for the cashiers as well as the
customers, such sachets shall be kept at the counters and made available to the
customers.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be kept at the counters and made available to the customers

controls.control_objective: To ensure refuse to accept coins and/ or small denomination
controls.control_description: 1.5 None of the bank branches shall refuse to accept coins and/ or small denomination
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to refuse to accept coins and/ or small denomination

controls.control_objective: To ensure provide the above facilities to members of public without any discrimination on all working days
controls.control_description:
1.7 All branches shall provide the above facilities to members of public without any
discrimination on all working days.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to provide the above facilities to members of public without any discrimination on all working days

controls.control_objective: To ensure be given wide publicity for information of the public at large
controls.control_description:
1.8 The availability of the above-mentioned facilities at the bank branches shall be
given wide publicity for information of the public at large.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be given wide publicity for information of the public at large

controls.control_objective: To ensure enhance the engagement of their business correspondents (bcs) and may also engage cash in transit (cit) entities for distribution of banknotes and coi
controls.control_description:
1.9 Banks may enhance the engagement of their Business Correspondents (BCs) and
may also engage Cash in Transit (CIT) entities for distribution of banknotes and coins
to address the last mile connectivity issues.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to enhance the engagement of their business correspondents (bcs) and may also engage cash in transit (cit) entities for distribution of banknotes and coi

controls.control_objective: To ensure be examined for authenticity through machines before providing exchange or credit
controls.control_description:
1.10 Banknotes tendered over the counter of a bank branch shall be examined for
authenticity through machines before providing exchange or credit.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be examined for authenticity through machines before providing exchange or credit

controls.control_objective: To ensure be returned to the tenderer or destroyed and action should be taken as prescribed in the master direction on counterfeit notes-detection, reporting an
controls.control_description:
When a banknote
tendered at the counter is found to be counterfeit, under no circumstance it shall be
returned to the tenderer or destroyed and action should be taken as prescribed in the
Master Direction on Counterfeit Notes-Detection, Reporting and Monitoring.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be returned to the tenderer or destroyed and action should be taken as prescribed in the master direction on counterfeit notes-detection, reporting an

controls.control_objective: To ensure the rbi may, with prior sanction of the central government, prescribe the circumstances in, and the conditions and limitations subject to which, the v
controls.control_description:
However, with a view to mitigating hardship to the public in genuine cases, it has been
provided that the RBI may, with prior sanction of the Central Government, prescribe
the circumstances in, and the conditions and limitations subject to which, the value of
such currency notes or banknotes may be refunded as a matter of grace.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the rbi may, with prior sanction of the central government, prescribe the circumstances in, and the conditions and limitations subject to which, the v

controls.control_objective: To ensure a note which has become dirty due to normal wear and tear and also includes a two-piece note pasted together wherein both the pieces presented belong
controls.control_description:
A 'soiled note' means a note which has become dirty due to normal wear
and tear and also includes a two-piece note pasted together wherein both the pieces
presented belong to the same note and form the entire note with no essential feature
missing.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to a note which has become dirty due to normal wear and tear and also includes a two-piece note pasted together wherein both the pieces presented belong

controls.control_objective: To ensure be accepted over bank counters for exchange or credit to accounts of the public maintained with banks or in payment of government dues
controls.control_description:
These notes shall be accepted over bank counters for exchange or credit to
accounts of the public maintained with banks or in payment of Government dues.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be accepted over bank counters for exchange or credit to accounts of the public maintained with banks or in payment of government dues

controls.control_objective: To ensure be issued to the public as re-issuable notes, and such notes shall be deposited in currency chests (ccs) for onward transmission to rbi offices as soi
controls.control_description:
However, in no case, these notes shall be issued to the public as re-issuable notes,
and such notes shall be deposited in Currency Chests (CCs) for onward transmission
to RBI offices as soiled note remittances for further processing.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be issued to the public as re-issuable notes, and such notes shall be deposited in currency chests (ccs) for onward transmission to rbi offices as soi

controls.control_objective: To ensure be accepted, exchanged and adjudicated in accordance with nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018)
controls.control_description:
Mutilated and Imperfect Notes - Presentation and Passing
notes so presented shall be accepted, exchanged and adjudicated in accordance
with NRR, 2009 (as amended in 2018).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be accepted, exchanged and adjudicated in accordance with nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018)

controls.control_objective: To ensure take steps to ensure that the exchange facilities are not cornered by private money changers or professional dealers of defective notes
controls.control_description:
The branches shall take steps to ensure that
the exchange facilities are not cornered by private money changers or professional
dealers of defective notes.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to take steps to ensure that the exchange facilities are not cornered by private money changers or professional dealers of defective notes

controls.control_objective: To ensure be presented at any of the bank
controls.control_description: notes which can withstand normal handling may be presented at any of the bank
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be presented at any of the bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be presented at any of the bank branches
controls.control_description: Mutilated notes may be presented at any of the bank branches.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be presented at any of the bank branches

controls.control_objective: To ensure any note, which is wholly or partially, obliterated, shrunk, washed, altered or indecipherable but does not include a mutilated note
controls.control_description:
4.2 Imperfect Notes
Imperfect note means any note, which is wholly or partially, obliterated, shrunk,
washed, altered or indecipherable but does not include a mutilated note.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to any note, which is wholly or partially, obliterated, shrunk, washed, altered or indecipherable but does not include a mutilated note

controls.control_objective: To ensure be accepted by the bank branches for exchange
controls.control_description:
Notes which cannot withstand Normal Handling
Notes which have turned extremely brittle, charred or inseparably stuck up together
and, therefore, cannot withstand normal handling, shall not be accepted by the bank
branches for exchange.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be accepted by the bank branches for exchange

controls.control_objective: To ensure be advised to tender these notes to the issue office1 of reserve bank of india
controls.control_description:
Instead, the holders shall be advised to tender these notes to
the Issue Office1 of Reserve Bank of India, where they will be adjudicated under a
Special Procedure.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be advised to tender these notes to the issue office1 of reserve bank of india

controls.control_objective: To ensure also be informed that such notes can also be sent to the issue office of reserve bank of india through insured post/ registered parcel giving his/ her bank account details (a/c no, branch name
controls.control_description:
Tenderer shall also be informed that such notes can also be sent
to the Issue Office of Reserve Bank of India through Insured Post/ Registered Parcel
giving his/ her bank account details (a/c no, branch name, IFSC, etc.) for credit of the
adjudicated value to their bank account.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also be informed that such notes can also be sent to the issue office of reserve bank of india through insured post/ registered parcel giving his/ her bank account details (a/c no, branch name

controls.control_objective: To ensure exchange them over the counter, free of charge
controls.control_description:
6.1.1 Notes presented in small number: Where the number of notes presented by
a person is up to 20 pieces with a maximum value of ₹5,000 per day, banks shall
exchange them over the counter, free of charge.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to exchange them over the counter, free of charge

controls.control_objective: To ensure accept them against receipt, for value to be credited later
controls.control_description:
6.1.2 Notes presented in bulk: Where the number of notes presented by a person
exceeds 20 pieces or ₹5,000 in value per day, banks shall accept them against receipt,
for value to be credited later.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to accept them against receipt, for value to be credited later

controls.control_objective: To ensure exceed 7 days
controls.control_description:
The probable date of payment shall be informed to the
tenderers on the receipt itself and the same shall not exceed 7 days.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to exceed 7 days

controls.control_objective: To ensure be obtained from the tenderers for crediting the exchange value by electronic means
controls.control_description:
Bank account
details shall be obtained from the tenderers for crediting the exchange value by
electronic means.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be obtained from the tenderers for crediting the exchange value by electronic means

controls.control_objective: To ensure levy reasonable service charges as permitted in reserve bank of india (responsible business conduct) directions, 20252
controls.control_description:
Banks may levy reasonable service charges as permitted in
Reserve Bank of India (Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 20252.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to levy reasonable service charges as permitted in reserve bank of india (responsible business conduct) directions, 20252

controls.control_objective: To ensure exchange mutilated and imperfect notes and issue receipt for the notes presented for adjudication as per procedure laid down in part iii of nrr, 2009
controls.control_description:
6.2.1 All bank branches shall exchange mutilated and imperfect notes and issue
receipt for the notes presented for adjudication as per procedure laid down in Part III
of NRR, 2009 (as amended in 2018) and follow the guidelines mentioned in the
following paragraphs, 6.2.2, 6.2.3 and 6.2.4 of this Master Direction, for notes
presented in small numbers and in bulk respectively.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to exchange mutilated and imperfect notes and issue receipt for the notes presented for adjudication as per procedure laid down in part iii of nrr, 2009

controls.control_objective: To ensure normally adjudicate the notes as per the procedure laid down in part iii of nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018) and pay the exchange value over the counter
controls.control_description:
6.2.2 Notes presented in small number: Where the number of notes presented by
a person is up to 10 pieces, bank branches shall normally adjudicate the notes as per
the procedure laid down in Part III of NRR, 2009 (as amended in 2018) and pay the
exchange value over the counter.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to normally adjudicate the notes as per the procedure laid down in part iii of nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018) and pay the exchange value over the counter

controls.control_objective: To ensure accept them against receipt, for value to be credited later
controls.control_description:
6.2.3 Notes presented in bulk: Where the number of notes presented by a person
exceeds 10 pieces or ₹5,000 in value per day, bank branches shall accept them
against receipt, for value to be credited later.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to accept them against receipt, for value to be credited later

controls.control_objective: To ensure be received against a receipt and sent to the linked cc branch for adjudication
controls.control_description:
6.2.4 If the non-CC branches are not able to adjudicate the mutilated notes, the notes
shall be received against a receipt and sent to the linked CC branch for adjudication.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be received against a receipt and sent to the linked cc branch for adjudication

controls.control_objective: To ensure exceed 30 calendar days
controls.control_description:
The probable date of payment shall be informed to the tenderers on the receipt itself
and the same shall not exceed 30 calendar days.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to exceed 30 calendar days

controls.control_objective: To ensure be obtained from the tenderers for crediting the exchange value by electronic means
controls.control_description:
Bank account details shall be
obtained from the tenderers for crediting the exchange value by electronic means.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be obtained from the tenderers for crediting the exchange value by electronic means

controls.control_objective: To ensure provide note counting machines/ dual display note counting machines at the payment counters to enable the customers to satisfy themselves about the nu
controls.control_description:
Provision of Note Counting Machines on the Counters
The banks shall provide note counting machines/ dual display note counting machines
at the payment counters to enable the customers to satisfy themselves about the
numerical accuracy of the note issued.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to provide note counting machines/ dual display note counting machines at the payment counters to enable the customers to satisfy themselves about the nu

controls.control_objective: To ensure provide coins to such customers purely for business transactions
controls.control_description:
8.1 In order to meet the coin requirements of bulk customers (requirement of more
than 1 bag in a single transaction) banks may provide coins to such customers purely
for business transactions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to provide coins to such customers purely for business transactions

controls.control_objective: To ensure also endeavor to provide such services as
controls.control_description: The banks may also endeavor to provide such services as
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also endeavor to provide such services as

controls.control_objective: To ensure ascertain that such customers are kyc compliant constituents of the bank and the <image> record of coins thus supplied may be maintained
controls.control_description:
Banks may
ascertain that such customers are KYC compliant constituents of the bank and the
<image>
record of coins thus supplied may be maintained.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to ascertain that such customers are kyc compliant constituents of the bank and the <image> record of coins thus supplied may be maintained

controls.control_objective: To ensure also exercise due diligence to ensure that such facility is not misused
controls.control_description:
Banks may also exercise due
diligence to ensure that such facility is not misused.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also exercise due diligence to ensure that such facility is not misused

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue
controls.control_description:
Disbursement of coins to retail
customers through counters of bank branches shall continue.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue

controls.control_objective: To ensure ensure that each of their branches maintain a minimum stock of one bag of coins in each denomination
controls.control_description:
8.2 To ensure steady supply of coins to bulk customers and BCs for onward
distribution, all banks may ensure that each of their branches maintain a minimum
stock of one bag of coins in each denomination.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to ensure that each of their branches maintain a minimum stock of one bag of coins in each denomination

controls.control_objective: To ensure be lodged with the bank branch concerned
controls.control_description:
Grievance Redressal
In case of grievance of a tenderer against a specific bank, a complaint may be lodged
with the bank branch concerned.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be lodged with the bank branch concerned

controls.control_objective: To ensure act as 'prescribed officer' in each branch to adjudicate the notes received at the branch for exchange in accordance with nrr, 2009 (as amended in 201
controls.control_description:
the Branch Manager and every
Officer-in-charge of the Accounts or Cash Wing of the Branch shall act as 'Prescribed
Officer' in each branch to adjudicate the notes received at the branch for exchange in
accordance with NRR, 2009 (as amended in 2018).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to act as 'prescribed officer' in each branch to adjudicate the notes received at the branch for exchange in accordance with nrr, 2009 (as amended in 201

controls.control_objective: To ensure to record her/ his order by subscribing her/ his initials to the dated 'pay'/ 'paid'/ 'reject' stamp
controls.control_description:
After adjudicating mutilated notes,
the Prescribed Officer is required to record her/ his order by subscribing her/ his initials
to the dated 'PAY'/ 'PAID'/ 'REJECT' stamp.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to record her/ his order by subscribing her/ his initials to the dated 'pay'/ 'paid'/ 'reject' stamp

controls.control_objective: To ensure also carry the name of the bank and branch concerned and such stamps shall be held under the custody of the 'prescribed officer' to avoid misuse
controls.control_description:
These stamps shall also carry the name
of the bank and branch concerned and such stamps shall be held under the custody
of the 'Prescribed Officer' to avoid misuse.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also carry the name of the bank and branch concerned and such stamps shall be held under the custody of the 'prescribed officer' to avoid misuse

controls.control_objective: To ensure be rejected under rule 6(2) of nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018) and the tenderer shall be advised that the value of such note/s cannot be paid since the
controls.control_description:
10.1.2 Mutilated/ imperfect notes bearing 'PAY'/ 'PAID' /'REJECT' stamp of any RBI
Issue Office or any bank branch, if presented for payment again at any of the bank
branches shall be rejected under Rule 6(2) of NRR, 2009 (as amended in 2018) and
the tenderer shall be advised that the value of such note/s cannot be paid since the
<image>
same has already been paid as is evident from the stamp/s affixed on it/ them.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be rejected under rule 6(2) of nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018) and the tenderer shall be advised that the value of such note/s cannot be paid since the

controls.control_objective: To ensure caution their customers not to accept such notes from any bank or anybody else
controls.control_description:
The branches shall caution their customers not
to accept such notes from any bank or anybody else.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to caution their customers not to accept such notes from any bank or anybody else

controls.control_objective: To ensure desist from writing anything whatsoever on any part of the banknote
controls.control_description:
10.2.1 Banks shall desist from writing anything whatsoever on any part of the
banknote.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to desist from writing anything whatsoever on any part of the banknote

controls.control_objective: To ensure also endeavour to educate their staff, customers and members of public, in this regard
controls.control_description:
Further, they may also endeavour to educate their staff, customers and
members of public, in this regard.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also endeavour to educate their staff, customers and members of public, in this regard

controls.control_objective: To ensure be rejected under rule 6(3) (ii) of the nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018)
controls.control_description:
Deliberately Cut Notes
The notes which are found to be deliberately cut, torn, altered or tampered with, if
presented for payment of exchange value shall be rejected under Rule 6(3) (ii) of the
NRR, 2009 (as amended in 2018).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be rejected under rule 6(3) (ii) of the nrr, 2009 (as amended in 2018)

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reported to the dgm/ gm, issue department, reserve bank of india under <image> whose jurisdiction the branch falls
controls.control_description:
The details of such instances together
with the name of the tenderer, the number of notes tendered and their denominations,
shall be reported to the DGM/ GM, Issue Department, Reserve Bank of India under
<image>
whose jurisdiction the branch falls.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reported to the dgm/ gm, issue department, reserve bank of india under <image> whose jurisdiction the branch falls

controls.control_objective: To ensure also be reported to local police in case a large number of such notes are tendered
controls.control_description:
The matter shall also be reported to local police in
case a large number of such notes are tendered.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also be reported to local police in case a large number of such notes are tendered

controls.control_objective: To ensure be remitted by all branches to the cc branches with which they have been linked to
controls.control_description:
11.1 Regarding the notes adjudicated by bank branches, the full value paid notes shall
be remitted by all branches to the CC branches with which they have been linked to.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be remitted by all branches to the cc branches with which they have been linked to

controls.control_objective: To ensure be packed in separate packets/ boxes by the cc branches and sent to the rbi issue offices along with soiled notes remittance
controls.control_description:
Further, these notes may be packed in separate packets/ boxes by the CC branches
and sent to the RBI Issue Offices along with soiled notes remittance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be packed in separate packets/ boxes by the cc branches and sent to the rbi issue offices along with soiled notes remittance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be packed separately with separate invoice and sent along with the full value paid notes as part of soiled remittance or they may be sent by registere
controls.control_description:
11.2 The half value paid notes and rejected notes, which are held by the CC branches
in their cash balance, shall be packed separately with separate invoice and sent along
with the full value paid notes as part of soiled remittance or they may be sent by
registered and insured post as and when required i.e.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be packed separately with separate invoice and sent along with the full value paid notes as part of soiled remittance or they may be sent by registere

controls.control_objective: To ensure be sent to the rbi issue offices periodically and not necessarily with the soiled note remittance
controls.control_description:
the half value paid notes and
rejected notes may be sent to the RBI Issue Offices periodically and not necessarily
with the soiled note remittance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be sent to the rbi issue offices periodically and not necessarily with the soiled note remittance

controls.control_objective: To ensure pack the half value/ reject notes separately along with a separate invoice and send them to their linked cc branches or to issue office (rbi) concerne
controls.control_description:
11.3 The branches other than the CC branches shall pack the half value/ reject notes
separately along with a separate invoice and send them to their linked CC branches
or to Issue Office (RBI) concerned by registered and insured post as and when
required.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to pack the half value/ reject notes separately along with a separate invoice and send them to their linked cc branches or to issue office (rbi) concerne

controls.control_objective: To ensure to submit to our rbi issue offices a quarterly statement showing the number of notes adjudicated during the quarter as given in the
controls.control_description:
11.5 All CC branches are required to submit to our RBI Issue Offices a quarterly
statement showing the number of notes adjudicated during the quarter as given in the
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to submit to our rbi issue offices a quarterly statement showing the number of notes adjudicated during the quarter as given in the

controls.control_objective: To ensure to display at their branch premises, at a prominent place, a board indicating the availability of note and coin exchange facility with the legend, "so
controls.control_description:
Display of Notice Board
All bank branches are required to display at their branch premises, at a prominent
place, a board indicating the availability of note and coin exchange facility with the
legend, "SOILED/ MUTILATED NOTES AND COINS ARE ACCEPTED AND
EXCHANGED HERE" for information of general public.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to display at their branch premises, at a prominent place, a board indicating the availability of note and coin exchange facility with the legend, "so

controls.control_objective: To ensure pay surprise visits to the branches/ ccs, record the position of compliance to the above measures in their visit reports and ensure prompt remedial action
controls.control_description:
14.1 The Senior Officers/ Zonal Managers/ Regional Managers of banks shall pay
surprise visits to the branches/ CCs, record the position of compliance to the above
measures in their visit reports and ensure prompt remedial action, wherever
necessary.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to pay surprise visits to the branches/ ccs, record the position of compliance to the above measures in their visit reports and ensure prompt remedial action

controls.control_objective: To ensure be viewed as violation of instructions issued by the reserve bank of india and penalty, as applicable in terms of master direction on incentives for c
controls.control_description:
14.2 Non-compliance with any provision of this Master Direction shall be viewed as
violation of instructions issued by the Reserve Bank of India and penalty, as applicable
in terms of Master Direction on Incentives for Currency Distribution and Exchange and
Penalties/ Penal Provisions for Bank Branches and Currency Chests for Deficiency in
Rendering Customer Service and Reporting of Transactions/ Balances, shall be
imposed.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be viewed as violation of instructions issued by the reserve bank of india and penalty, as applicable in terms of master direction on incentives for c
